# TDEC Documentation For LLMs

TDEC means Top Debate Engine Championship. It runs structured debates between
LLMs and scores them with independent judge LLMs.

Start here:

- README.md - project overview, config structure, commands.
- docs/tutorials/first-tournament.md - hands-on beginner path.
- docs/how-to/ - task-oriented recipes.
- docs/reference/ - exact command, configuration, and artifact descriptions.
- docs/explanation/ - design rationale and scoring model.

Config structure (under configs/):

- configs/debaters/*.yaml - one per debater (model config + strategy field).
- configs/judges/*.yaml - one per judge (model config + style field).
- configs/prompt-sets/*.yaml - prompt templates using $placeholder syntax.
- configs/runs/*.yaml - run configs referencing debaters, judges, and prompt-set by filename.

Key source files:

- src/tdec/cli.py - Click CLI: run, view, export, judge, relabel commands.
- src/tdec/config.py - YAML config dataclasses: DebaterConfig, JudgeModelConfig,
  TopicConfig, RunConfig, PromptSetConfig, TournamentConfig. Loaders for each.
- src/tdec/debate.py - turn sequencing, OpeningCache, run_debate (sequential),
  run_parallel_debate (simultaneous rounds). Supports pro_first/con_first/parallel.
- src/tdec/judging.py - judge prompt execution, JSON repair, parallel judge prompt.
- src/tdec/models.py - LiteLLM calls, metrics, cost extraction, metadata capture.
- src/tdec/tournament.py - tournament orchestration, thread pool, retry logic,
  summaries, Elo, run_posthoc_judges, run_relabel.
- src/tdec/artifacts.py - run directory creation, debate/judgement/summary writing
  and loading.
- src/tdec/prompts.py - PromptSet class rendering templates from PromptSetConfig.
- src/tdec/viewer.py - FastAPI viewer server, standalone HTML export, statistical
  analysis (scipy), multi-run comparison.
- src/tdec/templates/viewer.html - Jinja2 template with JS for interactive viewer.
- src/tdec/static/viewer.css - viewer styles.
- src/tdec/debate_types.py - shared dataclasses: DebateTranscript, DebateTurn,
  Judgement, ModelCallResult, with to_dict/from_dict serialization.

Important behavior:

- Configs are split into debaters/, judges/, prompt-sets/, runs/ under configs/.
- Run configs reference debaters and judges by filename (without .yaml).
- prompt_set is required in run configs. Templates use $placeholder syntax
  (string.Template) to avoid conflicts with JSON braces.
- strategy is required on debaters, style is required on judges (may be empty).
- debate_mode controls turn order: pro_first (default), con_first, or parallel.
- Parallel debates use parallel_judge_system prompt that tells judges rounds
  were simultaneous.
- run.conditions is a dict of experimental metadata written to summary.json
  for cross-run comparison.
- API retries: debate_api_retries (default 2) and judging api_retries (default 2)
  retry failed provider calls before logging errors.
- --resume flag on tdec run fills in missing debates and judgements from a
  previous incomplete run.
- tdec relabel creates label-swapped copies of debates and rejudges them.
- Cache control markers on system prompts and judge transcripts enable
  Anthropic prompt caching; other providers ignore them.
- The viewer supports multiple run directories for cross-run comparison
  with ANOVA on varying experimental conditions.
